parsed_questions.question_text: A) Which compound is more susceptible to electrophilic aromatic substitution? (Shown: aniline (NH2 bonded to benzene) and anilinium ion (NH3+ bonded to benzene)) B) Which carbonyl compound is more susceptible to nucleophilic attack? (Shown: cyclohexanone and pentanal) C) What is the purpose of the FeBr3 catalyst in an electrophilic aromatic substitution halogenation? i. It serves as a radical initiator ii. It destabilizes the carbocation intermediate iii. It acts as a Lewis acid to activate Br2 D) Rank in order of reactivity for electrophilic aromatic substitution (1 = most reactive) for these compounds: benzene, chlorobenzene, N-methylbenzamide benzene, benzamide (notations as visible). E) Rank in order of acidity (1 = most acidic) for the following phenol derivatives: phenol, 4-nitrophenol, 3-nitrophenol, 4-aminophenol.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: A) aniline; B) pentanal; C) iii; D) 1=benzamide, 2=benzene, 3=chlorobenzene, 4=N-methylbenzamide; E) 1=4-nitrophenol, 2=3-nitrophenol, 3=phenol, 4=4-aminophenol

parsed_questions.question_text: Draw the structure of the MAJOR product of the following reactions. If there is no reaction, write "NR". A) Reaction of 1,3,5-trimethylbenzene with HNO3/H2SO4. B) Reaction of benzene with isopropyl chloride and AlCl3. C) Reaction of 1,3-dichloro-2,4-dinitrobenzene with cyclohexylamine. D) Reaction of 1,4-dioxan-2-one (cyclic ester) with LiAlH4.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: A) 1,3,5-trimethyl-2-nitrobenzene major product by sterics; B) 1-isopropyl-3,5-dimethylbenzene (major by rearrangement), 1,3,5-trimethyl-2-isopropylbenzene also acceptable; C) No aromatic substitution (no aromatic substitution product) as major; D) 1,6-hexanediol (reduction of lactone to diol)

parsed_questions.error_comment: Task requires drawing and is not text-oriented; answer contains hand-drawn chemical structures not convertible to text or SMILES.

parsed_questions.question_text: Provide the necessary reagents for the following transformations. More than one step may be required. A) Transform cyclopentanone to cyclopentylamine (structure of cyclopentanone and cyclopentylamine shown). B) Transform benzene to 1-phenylpropan-1-ol (structures of benzene and 1-phenylpropan-1-ol shown). C) Transform 1-butanol to butanoic acid (structures of 1-butanol and butanoic acid shown). D) Transform cyclohexanol to cyclohexanecarbaldehyde (structures of cyclohexanol and cyclohexanecarbaldehyde shown).
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: A) 1) N2H2, CH2Cl2, CH3Br 2) NaBH4 or LiAlH4; B) 1) CH3CH2COCl, AlCl3 2) NaBH4 or LiAlH4; C) Na2Cr2O7 / H2SO4; D) PCC

parsed_questions.question_text:
The equilibrium constant for hydration of acetone is 2 x 10^-3, which translates into a small percent conversion to the following hydrate: acetone (CH3COCH3) equilibrates with its geminal diol hydrate (CH3C(OH)2CH3). The equilibrium constant Keq = 2 x 10^-3. Would you expect the equilibrium constant for hexafluoroacetone (CF3COCF3) to be smaller or larger than that for acetone?
Choose:
A) Keq greater than 2 x 10^-3
B) Keq less than 2 x 10^-3

Task requires drawing and is not text-oriented

parsed_questions.question_text: Draw a synthesis of the following product (a cyclohexene ring with a propyl substituent) from the given starting materials (cyclohexanone and 1-bromopropane). Additional reagents will be necessary. Each step in your synthesis must be clearly shown, but no mechanism is required.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: Convert 1-bromopropane to the corresponding triphenylphosphonium salt with PPh3; then treat with nBuLi to form the ylide; finally perform a Wittig reaction with cyclohexanone to yield the product alkene.

parsed_questions.question_text: Furan is readily acetylated with acetic anhydride and a Lewis acid such as BF3. Predict the major regioisomer produced in this reaction and justify your reasoning with resonance structures. The reaction scheme shows furan reacting with acetic anhydride and BF3 to give an acetylated furan product. Provide resonance structures explaining substitution positions.
parsed_questions.canonical_answer: The acetyl group substitutes at the 2-position of furan; resonance structures show stabilization of positive charge at the 2-position intermediate favored.
